Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The user wants me to act as an LLM analyzing a process mining dataset specifically a PostgreSQL database schema and sample data I need to identify anomalies undesirable behaviors in the provided order event log data hypothesize causes and write SQL queries to investigate those hypotheses

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Schema order event log The core process data Case ID Event ID Activity Timestamp Resource Info orders Case attributes Customer ID Type Value resources Resource attributes Role Department

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Normal Process Flow 1 Register Order Sales 2 Perform Credit Check Finance 3 Validate Stock Warehouse 4 Confirm Shipment Logistics 5 Ship Goods Logistics 6 Issue Invoice Finance 7 Receive Payment Finance Key constraints implied Sequence matters Credit check usually comes before invoicing payment Stock validation usually comes before shipping Shipping usually happens after confirmation

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Analyze the Sample Data Case by Case Case 1001 Looks perfect Follows the standard flow exactly Baseline Reg Credit Stock Confirm Ship Invoice Pay Case 1002 Looks weird Flow Reg Confirm Ship Credit Stock Invoice Pay Anomaly 1 Confirm Shipment happened before Validate Stock or Credit Check Anomaly 2 Ship Goods happened before Validate Stock How do you ship if you havent checked stock Hypothesis Rush job System glitch allowing out of order execution Priority order bypassing checks Case 1003 Looks weird Flow Reg Credit Ship Invoice Confirm Pay Anomaly 1 Missing Validate Stock Or maybe it was skipped Anomaly 2 Ship Goods happened before Confirm Shipment You shipped it then confirmed it later Anomaly 3 Issue Invoice happened before Confirm Shipment less critical but odd Hypothesis Process deviation Maybe the warehouse acted too fast or logistics was delayed late confirmation Y Case 1004 Very weird Flow Reg Pay Invoice Confirm Ship Anomaly 1 Receive Payment before Issue Invoice Usually you invoice to get paid Unless its cash on delivery or prepayment but the order type is standard Anomaly 2 Receive Payment immediately after registration No credit check no stock validation Anomaly 3 Missing activities entirely No Credit Check No Validate Stock Did they just take money and run Hypothesis Data entry error Fraud A specific Prepaid workflow not captured in the standard definition Or a system bug where events are logged under wrong Case IDs

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Synthesize Findings Hypotheses Finding A Rework Out of Sequence Execution Cases 1002 1003 Logistics Warehouse steps happen before Finance Validation steps Hypothesis High priority orders order type priority in case 1002 might be forcing a bypass of standard controls Or specific resources eg LogisticsMgr 2 are aggressive Finding B Skipped Activities Cases 1003 1004 Case 1003 missing Validate Stock Case 1004 missing Credit Check Validate Stock Hypothesis Incomplete process execution Potential revenue leakage shipping without credit check or operational failure shipping without stock Finding C Reverse Financial Flow Case 1004 Payment received before Invoice issued Hypothesis Prepayment model not reflected in process map Or incorrect timestamp logging

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Drafting SQL Queries for Investigation Query 1 Identify deviations from the strict sequence Hard to do in pure SQL without LAG LEAD or self joins Lets use Window Functions LAG to find immediate predecessors that dont match the expected logic Query 2 Find cases missing mandatory steps Group by case id count distinct activities compare against list of expected activities Query 3 Analyze Payment before Invoice specifically Query 4 Correlate anomalies with Order Attributes is it because its priority Query 5 Correlate anomalies with Resources is it always LogisticsMgr 2

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Refining the Output Structure Section 1 Anomaly Identification Describe exactly what looks wrong in 1002 1003 1004 compared to 1001 Section 2 Hypotheses Why did this happen Priority handling Resource behavior Data quality Section 3 Investigative SQL Queries Query for Sequence Violations using LAG Query for Missing Activities Query for Time Duration outliers optional but good Query joining with Orders Resources to find patterns Root Cause Analysis

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Self Correction Refinement during drafting Initial thought Just write one big query Correction Better to write targeted queries for each hypothesis so the user can understand what is being checked SQL Specifics Ensure PostgreSQL syntax eg WITH clauses LAG OVER string aggregation if needed Data details Note that Case 1002 is Priority This strongly supports the hypothesis that priority orders skip queues steps Note Case 1004 has high value 3000 but skipped credit check this is risky

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Final Review against Prompt Identify anomalies Yes Hypothesize why Yes Propose SQL queries Yes No hints used I am generating them based on my analysis Self Correction on SQL Logic To check sequence violations effectively I need to define what a bad transition looks like Good Register Credit Bad Register Payment Case 1004 Bad Register Confirm Case 1002 I will construct a query that lists prev activity current activity pairs and flags unexpected ones based on the Assumed Normal Process
